title: Mystical Argiculture Infusion Crafting Automation
content:
Name "MA Infusion Automation"
-- "Automating Mystical Argiculture with for example AE2"
-- "Only one pattern at a time, set Blocking mode to not push unless inventory is empty."
-- Incomming "is the inventory where the ingredients are put."
-- InfusionPedistal and InfusionAltar "are self explanatory."
-- Outgoing "is where the result is sent."
 
Every 20 ticks do
    Input from Incomming
    Output 1 except 
        spawn_egg_configurable_bee,
        *_seed_base,
        *_augment,
        to each InfusionPedestal
    Output 
        spawn_egg_configurable_bee,
        *_seed_base,
        *_augment,
        to InfusionAltar
 
    If not some InfusionPedestal has = 1 item:: Then
        Input from InfusionAltar
        Output to Outgoing
    End
End

title: Workaround Fix: recurrence event
content:
<?php 

/*
 * Patch: recurrence not showing when events_list is filter by category
 */
add_filter('em_event_save','my_event_save', 100, 2);
function my_event_save( $result, $EM_Event ){
	global $wpdb;
	
	if ( !empty( $EM_Event->get_recurrence_set() ) ){
		$event_type = "single";
		$wpdb_results = $wpdb->query ( $wpdb->prepare("UPDATE ". EM_EVENTS_TABLE ." SET event_type = %s  WHERE event_id=%d", array($event_type, $EM_Event->event_id) ) );
	}
	
	return $result;
}

title: Vis - Blades
content:
Twice, I am forced to fight. Once to take down a single crazed Sextus who seems to have run far ahead of his comrades. Bloodlust lights his eyes, a fierce grin on his face as he chases terrified Octavii past the darkened alley where I hide. I almost let him go too, but can’t. My armour disintegrates into a cloud of spinning metal blades that I send at his back. His dying screams haunt me as I stumble on, ignoring the grateful shouts of the men and women I leave in my wake.

The second time, it is a dozen Septimii who sprint around a corner before I can conceal myself. They see me alone but approach smartly, cautiously, so that when my blades arrow toward them they are able to mostly absorb the attack, the triangles embedding in the wood of their shield wall.

It’s a good tactic; usually stone chips upon impact and the imbuing is lost. It’s especially true for Praetorians’ razors.

Unfortunately for them, metal has no such issues.

The Septimii realise they’re outmatched too late, their fear only exacerbated as Diago crashes into their line, snarling and knocking two of them over from the sheer force of his attack. One man’s throat is ripped clean out and as another goes to thrust his blade into my alupi’s side, I take him in the neck with a spinning shard. The remaining Septimii break, retreating. Diago kills another. I finish the rest.

It’s over in less than a minute. No time to consider. Bodies strewn. Blood coats the triangles as I bring them back to my chest. Bile in my stomach, but it was them or me.

I end the groans of the last surviving man, and press on.

The Strength of the Few Chapter 77

title: Untitled
Untitled
content:
She shook her head in response to his question. With a twisted grin on her face,
she hastily took her seat. She scowled at the ground deliriously, like a black
panther who had just had its collar removed. It was proof that she had
recognized Kurogiri as some sort of abnormal entity.
"The documents Mother sent me didn't have his photo. I may have made a
mistake letting Azaka handle this. I should've just taken care of it myself. No,
even if I did, I'd only end up another victim in his pile of stolen memories."
Mikiya could only sit there puzzled, contemplating what kind of metaphor
Touko was getting at when she mentioned stolen memories. Still, he felt that he
had to make sure.

Kara no Kyoukai: The Garden of Sinners, Oblivion Recording, Chapter 5

title: Untitled
content:
"Apparently he's her connection in the police force. I told him about my boss
when we met on New Year's, then he went all 'Wait, are you talking about
Aozaki Touko?!' on me. Touko said that she couldn't refuse an invite from an
employee's family, so they're out on a date right now. Basically, he used me as
bait so that he could get to her," he grumbled, clearly discontent.
It certainly didn't require a stretch of the imagination to believe that Daisuke
was one of Ms. Touko's sources of information. He was known as an oddball
even within the First Investigation Division, so it actually made quite a bit of
sense.

Kara no Kyoukai: The Garden of Sinners, Oblivion Recording, Chapter 5

title: Untitled
content:
“Are you sure? The monster in that box might be able to defeat me.”
“I refuse. This guy’s endless, if I let him go carelessly this entire apartment complex itself could
disappear. If I did something so flashy the Association would not stay silent. This time, I’d be in a
position to be pursued by the Association. I went through a lot of trouble to disappear, so I’m not
going to do anything that’ll get me found by those guys.”

Kara no Kyoukai: The Garden of Sinners, Volume 5, Paradox Spiral 6 /16

title: Untitled
content:
The Magus had fallen asleep, but his consciousness appeared in front of her, leaving his body on the
tenth floor.
Without shape or form, he had an unbroken view of the entire first floor lobby.
... On the first floor in the east building’s lobby were Aozaki Touko and the boy called Kokutou
Mikiya.
Aozaki Touko was nursing the boy who had fallen down, it appeared that Cornelius Alba was not
there.
The Magus thought, “So it turned out just like how I expected it to.” Then he nodded.
After confirming the outcome, the Magus began to restore his consciousness to his body on the
tenth floor.
However, she held him back from that.
“Where are you going Araya? Peeping is in poor taste.”
Like she was able to see the Magus who wasn’t there, Aozaki Touko turned around.
She was at the bottom of the stairs. The Magus’s formless consciousness was at the top of the stairs.
Strangely, the two confronted each other in the same positions as before.
“... I knew you were going to have some way to execute Alba, I didn’t know there was another
Aozaki Touko. The heart I pierced was unquestionably the real thing, not an artificial object. Then,
you’re an imitation?”
The voice was only an echo. No, it wasn’t really a sound. Araya’s words only registered with
Aozaki Touko.

Kara no Kyoukai: The Garden of Sinners, Volume 5, Paradox Spiral 6 /16

title: Untitled
content:
Inside the bag there was a glow.

–Two eyes.

“I see,” Alba thought as he nodded.
He saw this bag earlier and had thought to himself it looked similar to something. It was really
simple. Why hadn’t he noticed it sooner?
The cube was too large to be called a bag. Didn’t it look exactly like the box that contains demons
straight from mythology?
Thus, an unknown black creature materialized from the box, black thorny tentacles reached out and
grabbed Cornelius Alba.
The creature pulled him straight into the box, starting from his feet he was chewed upwards by
thousands of small mouths. There were crunching sounds as he was being eaten alive. Just before he
was annihilated, when everything below his neck was gone, he made eye contact with the Puppeteer
who was looking down at him with a detached gaze.
As I die this horrific death she’s watching me, her eyes filling with ridicule.
That alone made him regret that there was no way he could have competed with her.
He recalled his last exchange of words with Araya. Araya must have anticipated this is what would
happen to Cornelius Alba.
As the last fragments of brain were being chewed.
... I failed.

Kara no Kyoukai: The Garden of Sinners, Volume 5, Paradox Spiral 6 /15

title: Untitled
content:
“Now, let’s return to the real issue at hand here, shall we? My boy’s life has already been in danger
long enough. It’s been about an hour since you started tormenting him as you pleased.
“Wh-what?”
It had been about an hour? Come to think of it, Aozaki did mention something about waking up
after her head had been crushed. If she had been asleep in her Workshop, then it certainly would
have taken an hour to get to this apartment complex. She couldn’t have gotten here in just a few
minutes, it would’ve been impossibly fast.
Suddenly, Alba looked at the boy who was lying on the stairs.
... The wounds on his legs were the same, but the back of the boy’s head, which he had struck many
times, was no longer bleeding.
This boy was unconscious solely due to of the bleeding from his legs.
“Ridiculous, what kind of Magic did you use Aozaki?”
The young man interrogated her weakly.
He had already lost his vigor. The difference between the two Magi had been shown to Alba, there
was no way he could have the will to attack Touko anymore.
“In a battle between Magi, a Magus shouldn’t talk recklessly about Magic. This is already my third
time coming to this lobby. I had constructed a Bounded Field from scratch in this room alone. Just
to be safe I took precautions and have prepared some tricks. For example, your thinking went blank
when you were surprised by Kokutou’s counterattack right? That was me lightly intervening with
your consciousness.”
“That time..?”
Alba groaned in frustration. It was true that when he had stopped the boy’s knife with the palm of
his hand, there was some sort of odd blankness.
From that time on, it had felt like he had been dreaming. He must have been standing there blankly
until the Magus Touko arrived.
“Haha, Hahaha, I see. You’ve had me in the palm of your hand since the beginning. I’m sure you
had fun, Aozaki. As much as I hate to admit it... It seems that I was the clown from the start.”

Kara no Kyoukai: The Garden of Sinners, Volume 5, Paradox Spiral 6 /15

title: Untitled
content:
“It’s a difference in the way we think, Alba. I don’t blame you for it, I’m envious of you. I don’t
know when it happened, it’s also unrecognizable to me. The current me awoke at the point in time
when my active self died. I gained the knowledge that the old Touko had recorded so it won’t be
any different than before. After this I guess I’ll produce a completely identical puppet and put it to
sleep.

Kara no Kyoukai: The Garden of Sinners, Volume 5, Paradox Spiral 6 /15

title: Untitled
content:
“It’s impossible for you to be here! This must be some kind of mistake! What are you doing here
Aozaki? I don’t know how you remain here, but the dead should behave like the dead and leave this
world!”
Alba swung his bloodstained arm in a straight line horizontally.
Blood scattered from the palm of his hand where he had been stabbed earlier by Kokutou Mikiya, it
flared up like gasoline when it made contact with the air. A visible spell made from the Magus’s
own blood and malice took the form of fire and engulfed the enemy who shouldn’t have been there.
However, the flames only lasted for a moment; they surrounded Aozaki Touko but dissipated before
they could even get close to her.
Touko brushed her hair back lightly and lit the cigarette in her mouth.

Kara no Kyoukai: The Garden of Sinners, Volume 5, Paradox Spiral 6 /15

title: Untitled
content:
“Or maybe you’re also an expert in constructing things. But if you can’t even protect yourself you
can’t really be called a Magus... Hmm, it seems Aozaki wasn’t a very good teacher. That’s right, she
was full of flaws to begin with. Did you know? In our Association the highest ranking practitioners
are given titles bearing colors. Among them, the three primary colors are proof of the best of their
time.
“Aozaki wanted the title of Blue, as her name suggests, but the Association didn’t give it to her. A
pure color doesn’t suit a woman who joined the Association for revenge after her younger sister
took her family inheritance away. Ironically, Aozaki received the title of Red, which contradicts her
last name. She was given a fittingly vulgar color for her first name, which contains the character for
orange, a color befitting an orange Magus! A dirty red can’t fully be the original red. Heh heh, what
a fitting title for that woman!”

Kara no Kyoukai: The Garden of Sinners, Volume 5, Paradox Spiral 6 /14

title: Untitled
content:
“It’s a pity Araya.”
“What–?”
Araya spoke to her, but she had already ceased all vital activity. The body of Aozaki Touko no
longer functioned as a living person.
All that remained to die was her brain. Without the flow of blood, her brain would soon deteriorate.
The knowledge and techniques she had accumulated would be completely lost.
The black Magus pulled his arm out of Touko’s body and placed his palm on her head. Grabbing her
face with an iron grip, he broke her neck with a snap. After that, he slowly ripped the head off of the
body and discarded the headless corpse on the floor.
Holding the severed head of his former comrade in one hand, the Magus turned on his heel.

Kara no Kyoukai: The Garden of Sinners, Volume 5, Spiral Paradox 9 /12

title: Untitled
content:
“I’ve also seen through the mechanisms of your familiar. That creature didn’t come out of the bag.
It was an image projected by the bag, wasn’t it?”
As Araya glared at the bag, it shattered into pieces. Inside the shattered bag was a machine equipped
with a lens and film. It was a was film projector making a whirring sound, whirr, whirr.
“A shadow-puppet familiar is it? I see. That would explain why it could nullify all attacks. Even if
the ether body projected into the atmosphere was destroyed, as long as the projector, the true body,
kept running, it could revive endlessly... It’s a shame, I have to remove such a genius.”

Kara no Kyoukai: The Garden of Sinners, Volume 5, Spiral Paradox 9 /12

title: Untitled
content:
And with that, Touko stepped away from the wall and continued walking. No, she had just started to
walk.

––Boom.

There was a sound. She thought it was a dull sound, it seemed like it was someone else’s concern.
Blood spilled from her mouth. With nowhere else to go, the blood that was expelled from her
organs could no longer be contained and was spat out.
With her vision beginning to get cloudy, she lowered her gaze and saw an arm.
Someone’s arm protruded from her chest.
“What a bizarre object,” Touko thought. A man’s thick arm extended from her chest holding a
plump heart in its grasp. Surely, it was her own heart.
She quickly came to the conclusion.
The enemy had appeared behind her and pierced her body; she was going to die.
“Kill in one shot, huh? I see, that was a good lesson.”

Kara no Kyoukai: The Garden of Sinners, Volume 5, Spiral Paradox 9 /12

title: Untitled
content:
“I told Alba this, but you don’t understand horror either. Do you know the three conditions to terrify
someone? One, the monster can’t speak. Two, the monster has to be unidentifiable. Three, it’s
meaningless if the monster can die.”
“––!”
Araya spun around.
The black cat he had crushed just moments before was now perched on the ceiling, as if nothing had
happened.
“Shuku!”
The man tightly clenched his palm directed toward the ceiling.
Thud, the space was suddenly compressed.
Shaking within that distortion, the black cat leaped down toward the Magus, opening its mouth with
a snap. Before the black Magus could evade he was nearly swallowed whole.
“Gah–” He let out a dull cry that sounded like it was in its death throes.
Squelch, a dull noise rang out.
It was different from the time with Shiki. Before the Magus could fight back, he had lost most of his
body.
Thud, only the Magus’s face and shoulders remained, falling to the ground. Even in death his face
was still full of anguish, as the piece of human flesh tumbled down the rest of the stairs.
After calmly observing the scene, Aozaki Touko briefly muttered.
“If you’re going to kill, do it in one shot. That’s how you pull off a surprise attack.”

Kara no Kyoukai: The Garden of Sinners, Volume 5, Spiral Paradox 9 /12

title: Untitled
content:
With a faint look of surprise, Araya turned around in an instant. Without hesitation, he spread his
outstretched palm and clenched it tightly once more.
Onn, a sound like a tremor could be heard.
Touko saw it, the space in front of Araya was collapsing further and further inward. The black cat
leaped upward before it could be compressed. As if gravity had reversed, the cat landed on the
ceiling and then glared at the Magus below.
“That’s enough.”
From beneath the black coat another arm extended, the fist tightly clenching.
The black cat and the spot on the ceiling on which it rested were crushed.
Bang, one corner of the ceiling caved in and the black cat was crushed, compressed beyond
recognition, and disappeared.
“Your puppet is gone now. ‘A Magus doesn’t need to be a strong fighter, they just need to create the
strongest thing with their skills’ ... Those were your words during our days at the academy. Indeed,
that is the case. A Puppeteer has lost the instant their puppet loses.”

Kara no Kyoukai: The Garden of Sinners, Volume 5, Spiral Paradox 9 /12

title: Untitled
content:
“Go!”
That being the case, she wouldn’t hold back either.
Suddenly, the black cat, which had been frozen until now, began once again to move. The paralysis
up to now had been an act. The cat pounced at Araya, who believing he was safe, had turned his
back on the cat.
“What?”

Kara no Kyoukai: The Garden of Sinners, Volume 5, Spiral Paradox 9 /12

title: Untitled
content:
And yet, the black Magus stopped at the entrance of the stairs, and held his arm out in front of him
moving slightly
“... Silence.”
A short murmur.
The Magus’s open palm clenched tightly.
It was a motion like crushing something in the palm of one’s hand.
Simultaneously, Touko’s body shook with a violent impact. Her coat, which blocked all forms of
magical interference with her Magic Circuits, was shredded to pieces and fell to the floor.
There had been an attack, it was an invisible impact.
Struck equally across her body from every direction, she then fell to her knees.
Touko instantly understood what the impact had been.
... Araya crushed the space in which Touko had been standing. It was as if her entire body had been
pressed down upon.
Touko’s tongue was silenced in disbelief. She had no knowledge of what Magecraft enabled such a
small gesture to manipulate space.
“... I got hit. Dammit, how many ribs did he break?”
Swallowing the blood pooling in her mouth, Touko assessed the damage to her body. Unlike Shiki,
Touko had no physical training, she had no way of knowing how many of her bones were broken.
All she understood was the fact that her coat had saved her.
If she were to take a hit like that again, she would certainly be crushed.

Kara no Kyoukai: The Garden of Sinners, Volume 5, Spiral Paradox 9 /12

title: Untitled
content:
The Rune Magecraft that Touko had mastered certainly had offensive capabilities. Runes are a form
of Magecraft that makes the meaning of the engraved letters a reality by carving a powerful seal
into a target. If Touko were to directly inscribe the Rune of Sowilō, which represented fire, onto
Araya’s body, then his body would burst into flames.
... The weak point, however, was that the runes had to be inscribed directly, overlapping letters from
a distance wouldn’t work on Magi. Indirect magical energy would be repelled by a Magus who had
direct magical energy coursing through their body.

Kara no Kyoukai: The Garden of Sinners, Volume 5, Spiral Paradox 9 /12
